description: Hard gates required before each stage can close. global_preflight applies to every stage and includes context confirmation plus requirement analysis artifacts.

semantic_checks: Workspace root has been confirmed in .pm-flow/project_config.json; the plugin workdir is under .pm-flow/work; existing human-owned directory structure is preserved.
If the current workspace path differs from the confirmed workspace binding, work stops until the human clarifies the workspace structure or approves a rebind.
Knowledge-base path has been confirmed in .pm-flow/project_config.json, or the human has explicitly confirmed that no external knowledge base is used with a recorded reason.
If the knowledge-base root, manifest, or confirmed source list is missing, moved, or inconsistent, work stops until the human confirms the knowledge-base boundary or approves a rebind.
Context intake has been confirmed: knowledge-base usage, entry points, applicable sources, non-applicable sources, rule strength, conflicts, and citation conclusion are explicit.
pm-requirement-analysis has produced a real requirement analysis, scenario catalog, and artifact plan; the files are not empty templates or generic summaries.
pm-requirement-analysis has produced use cases and end-to-end flow coverage, or explicit non-applicability reasons are recorded.
pm-metrics-definition has produced success, input, and guardrail metrics, or explicit non-applicability reasons are recorded.
Target users, usage scenarios, problem statement, and expected outputs are explicit.
The scenario catalog is detailed to role, client surface, trigger, user action, system response, fields, post-condition, exception, and acceptance lens.
Use cases are detailed to actor, goal, preconditions, trigger, main flow, alternate or exception flow, post-condition, fields, related metrics, and acceptance lens.
End-to-end flow coverage names start/end, actors, systems, handoffs, decision branches, state changes, data touched, and exception paths.
Scope, non-scope, key constraints, dependencies, and risks are explicit.
Confirmed facts, assumptions, and open questions are separated.
Original sources, knowledge-base materials, and historical documents have been checked for errors; any errors are classified as P3/P2/P1/P0.
P1/P0 source errors block downstream PRD, prototype, diagram, or review output until corrected or explicitly accepted by an owner.
The recommended flow path is recorded: new-platform, iteration, iteration-lite, or micro.
Requirement analysis level, PRD level recommendation, and artifact routing are recorded.

The artifact plan explicitly decides whether metrics definition, use cases, end-to-end flow, PRD, field dictionary, HTML prototype, prototype review artifact, Mermaid diagram, test scenarios, delivery check, and humanizer are needed.
Even direct user requests for PRD, prototype, diagram, or review output went through requirement analysis first.
A fixed full workflow was not run by default; unnecessary artifacts and premature technical stack choices are marked not applicable.
pm-research was used for research-stage opportunity, user, market, competitor, benchmark, metric-context, source-risk, assumption, and open-question analysis.
Competitors or benchmark solutions are covered.
Project constraints are listed: glossary, roles, system surfaces, and architecture boundaries.
Opportunity, user, market, and metric analysis has been completed or explicitly marked not applicable in the requirement analysis artifacts.
The output is registered in state.json.artifacts.
pm-boundary was used for boundary-stage scope, non-scope, stakeholders, roles, permissions, surfaces, system ownership, constraints, dependencies, assumptions, risks, and decisions.
Scope and non-scope are explicit.
Roles and responsibilities are aligned to the project role source when one exists.
Terms are aligned to the glossary when one exists.
Client surfaces and system ownership are explicit.
Key assumptions have been identified and prioritized, or the non-applicability reason is recorded.
The prototype opens offline without CDN dependencies.
Desktop and mobile views are available when required by the artifact plan.
Fields and rule documentation are linked when a review document is produced.
pm-html-prototype was used for HTML prototype output, unless the requirement analysis says no HTML prototype is needed.
Design guidelines or component-library rules from the knowledge base were applied; if missing, built-in baseline design assumptions are recorded.
When a frontend component library is specified, component mapping and version/API constraints were checked or marked not applicable.
Loading, empty, error, permission, timeout, duplicate-action, and concurrent states are covered when required by scenarios or acceptance criteria.
Terminology is aligned to the glossary when one exists.
pm-prd was used for PRD sizing and template selection.
The PRD level is explicit: L1, L2, or L3.
The PRD satisfies the required sections for its level.
Terminology is aligned to the glossary when one exists.
Roles are aligned to the role source when one exists.
Client surfaces and system ownership are explicit.
Acceptance criteria and exception flows are included.
Acceptance criteria are observable and testable, not subjective-only wording such as easy to use or beautiful.
Metrics definition, scenario catalog, use cases, and end-to-end flow are referenced or explicitly marked not applicable.
L2/L3 conditional branches have standalone Mermaid diagrams in the diagram directory, or the non-applicability reason is recorded.
L2/L3 fields, data sources, permissions, or write-back behavior have a field dictionary, or the non-applicability reason is recorded.
L3 or complex state-transition requirements include state-machine guidance, data dictionary, use cases, or an explicit non-applicability note.
Acceptance criteria can be converted into test scenarios, or the non-applicability reason is recorded.
The PRD has been polished with humanizer without changing facts, scope, or acceptance criteria, or the non-applicability reason is recorded.
Open questions are not written as facts.
Key decisions are recorded in state.json.decisions.
pm-review was used for general independent review-stage findings, decision checks, readiness conclusion, and close checklist.
Each finding is closed or explicitly recorded as unresolved.
Prototype review artifacts are generated only when requirement analysis says they are needed.
High-risk requirements have a risk review; code-shipping or AI-built app requirements have a delivery-check artifact plan, or the non-applicability reason is recorded.
Decision records are complete in state.json.decisions.
All previous stages are closed.
